incident_timeline.what_happened: The accused was charged with theft under Section 379 of the Penal Code. The incident occurred on October 12, 2023, at a residential property in Colombo. The prosecution alleges that the accused, a former domestic employee, stole valuable items. The defense claims the accused was at a temple during the time of the incident (alibi defense). No CCTV footage is available. A neighbor claims to have seen the accused leaving the area. The court must determine whether the circumstantial evidence is sufficient for conviction.

doubters_witnesses.name: Witness (Neighbor)
doubters_witnesses.role: Eyewitness claiming to see accused leaving scene
doubters_witnesses.doubt_factor: Visual identification occurred at night, may have limited reliability

doubters_witnesses.name: Temple Priest (Potential Alibi Witness)
doubters_witnesses.doubt_factor: May not be able to confirm exact timing of presence

prosecution_logic: Evidence supports prosecution case based on available facts
defense_logic: Defense presents counter-arguments based on available evidence
reasonable_doubt_factors: Factors creating reasonable doubt based on defense arguments

analyzed_case_file.final_judicial_opinion: The case requires careful evaluation of all evidence, witness credibility, and legal precedents. Both prosecution and defense present compelling arguments that must be weighed by the court.
